attention1.ptxt: When performing the following procedure, be sure to observe Precautions for Fuel System Service to reduce the risk of fire and personal injury.

s1.ptxt: Relieve fuel pressure from fuel feed line.
Insert hose of a hand operated pump into fuel filler hose (1) and pump out fuel in space “A” shown in figure.

attention2.ptxt: Do not force pump hose into fuel tank.

s1.ptxt: Hoist vehicle, and remove clamps (2), fuel filler hose (1) and breather hose (3) from fuel filler neck.
Disconnect quick joint (1) from fuel pipes (2).

attention1.ptxt: Never store fuel in an open container. Fuel in such container may easily cause fire or explosion.

s1.ptxt: Support fuel tank (1) with jack (2) and remove its mounting bolts.
Lower fuel tank a little to disconnect wire harness at connector (1), then remove fuel tank.

attention2.ptxt: Clean outside surfaces of pipe where joint is to be fitted, push joint into pipe till joint lock clicks, and check that pipes are connected securely. Failure to do this way result in fuel leakage.

s1.ptxt: Raise fuel tank (1) with jack (2) and connect fuel pump connector and clamp wire harness.
Install fuel tank to vehicle and tighten fuel tank bolts to specified torque.

attention2.ptxt: Fuel tank bolts are precoated with friction stabilizer. Never reuse removed bolts, otherwise bolts may loosen.

s1.ptxt: Connect fuel filler hose (1) and breather hose (2) to filler neck (3) as shown in figure, and clamp them securely.
For proper installation, refer to Fuel Hose Disconnection and Reconnection .
Connect fuel feed hose (1) and vapor hose (2) to each pipe as shown in figure, and clamp them securely.
With engine stopped, set ignition “ON” and check for fuel leaks.
